parameters.content:
**Step 1 — Configure & Scrape**
Edit `searchUrl` in `Configure Search` to target your niche (change the `q=` value). Set `maxResults` for sample size.

**Step 2 — Poll Until Complete**
Waits 20 seconds, then checks whether the Apify run has finished. Loops back if still running.

**Step 3 — Normalize, Score & Rank**
Weights profile fields (title 3×, skills 2×, bio 1×). Extracts unigrams, bigrams, and trigrams. Assigns tiers: **Essential** ≥50% · **High Demand** ≥25% · **Targeted** ≥10% · **Niche** <10%

nodes.name: Run Apify Actor
nodes.notes: Replace YOUR_NICHE_HERE in the URL with your niche, e.g. video+editor or shopify+developer

nodes.name: Save Run ID and Dataset ID
Save to Google Sheets
nodes.notes: Set your Google Sheet ID above. Column headers in Sheet1 must be: Rank, Keyword, Count, Type, Tier, Suggestion

parameters.content:
## Upwork Freelancer Profile Keyword Analyzer

Find out exactly which keywords top-rated Upwork freelancers use in their profiles 
so you can optimize yours for search visibility and client trust.

## How it works
THere’s a concise version:

The workflow scrapes up to 20 top Upwork profiles for any search term using Apify.
 It scores profile fields with weighted importance, giving titles 3× weight and skills 2× weight over bio text. 
It then measures keyword coverage across profiles, tracking how many profiles contain each keyword. 
Single words, two-word phrases, and three-word phrases are analyzed. 
Keywords are scored with a composite formula and grouped into tiers: Essential, High Demand, Targeted, and Niche. 
Final results are exported to Google Sheets with coverage data and placement suggestions.

## How to set up
1. Add your Apify API token to the `Run Apify Actor` node URL
2. Add your Google Sheets OAuth2 credential in n8n
3. Paste your Google Sheet ID into the `Save to Google Sheets` node
4. In `Configure Search`, update the `searchUrl` to your target niche

## How to customize
Change `maxResults` in `Configure Search` from 20 to 50+ for a larger keyword sample.
 Filter results in Google Sheets by the `Tier` column to focus on Essential and High Demand keywords first.

main.node: Save Run ID and Dataset ID
